hook:
X algorithm breakdown: replies > likes.

I tested this for 90 days.

Posts with 50 replies outperformed posts with 500 likes.

Here's what I learned about how the algorithm actually works:
tweets:
The hierarchy (based on my testing):

1. Replies (10x weight)
2. Retweets (5x weight)
3. Bookmarks (3x weight)
4. Quote tweets (3x weight)
5. Likes (1x weight)

A reply is worth 10 likes in the algorithm.

Why replies matter more:

1. Replies = time on platform (people read threads)
2. Replies = more content (users create it for free)
3. Replies = engagement bait (X wants this)
4. Replies = distribution (your post shows in repliers' feeds)

X optimizes for attention. Replies win.

The test:

Posted 2 tweets with same topic, same time of day.

Tweet A: Got 450 likes, 12 replies
Tweet B: Got 180 likes, 67 replies

Tweet B got 3x more impressions.

Replies > likes. Not close.

How to get more replies:

1. Ask a direct question
2. Make a controversial (but defensible) take
3. Share something incomplete (people fill gaps)
4. Post "what's your X?" content
5. End with "agree?" or "thoughts?"

Invite interaction. Don't just broadcast.

The self-reply hack:

Top reply is usually from the author.

Reply to your own tweet with:
- Additional context
- A CTA
- A link (replies don't get throttled like main tweets)

This also bumps your post in the algorithm.

Bookmarks are underrated:

Bookmark = "I want to come back to this"

X loves bookmarks because it means high-value content.

How to get bookmarks: Post actionable lists, templates, resources.

"Bookmark this" in the tweet helps (yes, it works).

What kills reach:

1. External links in main tweet (add in reply instead)
2. Editing within 30 minutes
3. Tweeting and immediately deleting/reposting
4. Too many hashtags (1-2 max)
5. Posting and going offline

Stay online for 30 minutes after posting. Reply to comments fast.

The engagement window:

First 30-60 minutes = critical.

If you get replies in that window, algorithm pushes to more feeds.

If not, post dies.

Strategy: Post when your audience is online. Reply to every comment immediately.
cta:
Grew from 2k to 14k followers using these patterns.

Follow @PRINTMAXXER for more algorithm breakdowns.

What's your best engagement hack? Reply below (see what I did there).
